method: static fetch + headless Chromium render (1440x900 + 390x844) + axe-core + Lighthouse + live link/header probes + source-mapping against /home/bryan/StencilMakerWebsite
deploymentModel: Astro 5 output:server (@astrojs/cloudflare v12) on Cloudflare Workers + Static Assets

findings.location: Live /artists and /artists/apply; homepage SponsoredArtist CTA + Footer 'Artist Directory'
findings.current: GET /artists -> 404, GET /artists/apply -> 404 (live); homepage links to /artists; live homepage copy is itself stale ('Argentina-born' vs source 'Patagonia-born')
findings.suggested: Redeploy master to production. Local dist/ already contains dist/artists/index.html + dist/artists/apply/index.html and a 5-URL sitemap; the build is correct, the deploy was never pushed.
findings.rationale: The entire featured-artist feature (commit d6f5df4, 2026-06-06) is committed but not live. The live homepage advertises a featured artist and links to /artists (SponsoredArtist 'Read the full feature' + Footer 'Artist Directory') which 404s, and the homepage itself runs older copy. Stale deploy is the single highest-leverage fix.

findings.location: Footer app badges + hero/CTA blocks ('APP STORE' / 'GOOGLE PLAY' / 'WEB APP')
findings.current: apps.apple.com/app/stencilmaker -> 404; play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.stencilmaker -> 404; app.thestencilmaker.com -> NXDOMAIN (no DNS, curl code 000)
findings.suggested: Replace the three SITE.appStoreUrl/playStoreUrl/webAppUrl placeholders with the real published listings; until the app ships, swap the dead CTAs for the working email-capture / 'coming soon' state.
findings.rationale: Every primary 'get the app' conversion CTA is dead. These are exactly the three '// TODO confirm' URLs in consts.ts. The whole site funnels to these buttons.

source.diff: appStoreUrl/playStoreUrl/webAppUrl (lines 31-33) are placeholders used by Footer.astro:31,39,47

findings.current: <meta property="og:image" content="https://thestencilmaker.com/assets/og-image.png"> -> 404
findings.suggested: Create a 1200x630 card at public/assets/og-image.png. Stopgap: point SITE.ogImage at an existing asset (e.g. /assets/sm-upscaled.png) so cards aren't blank.
findings.rationale: SITE.ogImage references a file that does not exist (consts.ts:25 even flags '// TODO: generate 1200x630 OG card'). Every share on social/Slack/iMessage renders a broken/blank card. Lighthouse SEO still 100 because og:image presence is not graded on resolvability.

findings.current: Cloudflare injects: Content-Signal: ai-train=no, and Disallow: / for ClaudeBot, GPTBot, Google-Extended, Bytespider, Applebot-Extended, meta-externalagent, CCBot, Amazonbot — directly above the site's own block which Allows all of them.
findings.suggested: Decide the policy and make robots.txt agree with itself. If GEO is intended (it is, per the repo): disable Cloudflare AI Crawl Control / Content-Signals managed robots.txt in the dashboard. If AI opt-out is intended: update public/robots.txt + SEO.astro meta + llms.txt to match.
findings.rationale: The repo robots.txt, the SEO.astro GEO meta tags (GPTBot/ClaudeBot/PerplexityBot index,follow), and llms.txt all deliberately ALLOW AI engines. A Cloudflare dashboard feature contradicts all three. Conflicting same-user-agent groups make crawler behaviour undefined and undermine the stated GEO strategy.

source.file: Cloudflare dashboard (AI Crawl Control / Content Signals)

findings.location: Gray/mono micro-text on obsidian background (text-neutral-500, text-stencil, text-xs/.font-mono)
findings.current: axe color-contrast (serious): 23 nodes home, 35 privacy, 43 terms (desktop); 14-17 nodes on mobile
findings.suggested: Lift the low end of the neutral ramp used for body/label text (e.g. text-neutral-400/300) and verify the indigo 'stencil' accent meets 4.5:1 on obsidian; re-run axe.
findings.rationale: Serious WCAG 1.4.3 failures on every page, dozens of nodes. Lighthouse a11y still 96 because it samples fewer nodes; axe (full DOM, real computed colors) is authoritative here.

findings.current: /privacy and /terms 307-redirect to /privacy/ and /terms/, but Footer/cross-page links use href="/privacy", href="/terms", href="/artists" (no slash) -> every click eats a 307
findings.suggested: Add trailing slashes to internal hrefs (Footer.astro:62-64; privacy.astro:199; terms.astro:189) to match the served canonical, and prefer a 308 (permanent) over Cloudflare's 307 for canonicalization.
findings.rationale: Minor latency + crawl-budget waste; 307 is 'temporary' so it doesn't consolidate signals the way 308/301 would.

findings.current: "It eliminated an hour of iPad tracing per client..." attributed to 'Elias Thorne / GHOST STUDIO // NYC' with portrait src=images.unsplash.com/photo-1507003211169-...
findings.suggested: Replace with a verifiable, consented testimonial (real artist + studio + real photo), or remove the section. Do not use a stock photo as a named person's portrait.
findings.rationale: Fabricated endorsement with a stock-photo 'portrait' presented as a real review — FTC deceptive-testimonial exposure and a trust risk, compounding the unsubstantiated 'TRUSTED BY MASTERS' marquee.

findings.current: "StencilMaker is the first AI architecture built for the demands of high-end tattoo craft." (meta description, hero, multiple JSON-LD blocks)
findings.suggested: Drop 'first' or add a defensible qualifier.
findings.rationale: Unprovable 'first' superlative repeated across visible copy + structured data.

findings.current: "stencil maker app" (two-word brand) in SITE.keywords
findings.suggested: Change to "stencilmaker app" for brand consistency (note: the keywords meta is ignored by Google; cosmetic).
findings.rationale: Brand spelled as two words, inconsistent with 'StencilMaker'.

findings.current: hero-graphic-light.png 2.39 MB, sm-upscaled.png 1.56 MB, gallery/dragon-2.png 1.30 MB (raw PNGs in public/). Lighthouse mobile LCP 26.1s (CLS 0.002, TBT 0ms, FCP 1.7s). Perf 74.
findings.suggested: Convert hero + gallery images to WebP/AVIF, add responsive srcset/sizes, and route them through astro:assets (<Image>) so they're optimized. The 2.4MB hero is the LCP element.
findings.rationale: Public-dir images bypass Astro image optimization and ship full-size; the 2.4MB hero dominates LCP under throttled mobile. CLS/TBT are already excellent, so this is purely a payload fix.

findings.current: axe scrollable-region-focusable (serious): the horizontally scrollable style rail has no keyboard access (no tabindex)
findings.suggested: Add tabindex="0" + an accessible name (aria-label) to #style-rail, or provide prev/next buttons.
findings.rationale: WCAG 2.1.1: keyboard users can't scroll the rail.

findings.location: StyleGallery heading vs card count (PRE-DEPLOY REGRESSION)
findings.current: StyleGallery.astro:43 hardcodes '10 Distinct Vocabularies.' but the styles array has only 4 cards (Sacred Geometry, Japanese, Fine Line, Hyper-Realism). Live (stale) site shows 10; current source was trimmed to 4.
findings.suggested: Before redeploy: either restore the other 6 styles or change the heading to match the real count. Redeploying as-is replaces today's consistent 10/10 with a contradictory 10-heading/4-cards.
findings.rationale: Redeploy (required for SW1) will INTRODUCE this contradiction unless fixed first.

findings.current: FAQ says Premium '...unlocks ... up to 10 stencils per day' with no caveat that the monthly plan is 5/day (Pricing.astro:39 -> '5 monthly · 10 yearly').
findings.suggested: Clarify: 'up to 10 stencils/day (5/day monthly, 10/day annual).'
findings.rationale: Search/AI surfaces the FAQ JSON-LD; monthly subscribers are over-promised their daily cap.

findings.current: "TRUSTED BY MASTERS — BUILT FOR THE CRAFT —" with no named, verifiable artists
findings.suggested: Substantiate with real artists or soften to 'BUILT FOR MASTERS'.
findings.rationale: Unsubstantiated social proof, paired with the fabricated testimonial (SW7).

findings.current: alt uses 'topographic', blurb uses 'topographical' on the same card
findings.suggested: Standardize to 'topographic'.
findings.rationale: Copy inconsistency within one component.

findings.current: privacy@stencilmaker.app (privacy.astro:749/756) and legal@stencilmaker.app (terms.astro:801/808) — domain @stencilmaker.app, not the canonical @thestencilmaker.com (consts.ts supportEmail/pressEmail).
findings.suggested: Confirm whether stencilmaker.app mailboxes exist & are monitored; if not, switch both to @thestencilmaker.com.
findings.rationale: Legal-contact emails on a different (possibly unowned/unmonitored) domain risk undeliverable rights/legal requests.

findings.current: Lists Vercel AI Gateway/Google Gemini, Sentry, PostHog — omits Cloudflare (hosting/analytics/Turnstile), Resend (transactional email), and Apple/Google as identity providers, all of which the site/app demonstrably use.
findings.suggested: Add Cloudflare, Resend, Turnstile, Apple, Google rows; split 'Vercel AI Gateway / Google Gemini' into two; add a cookieless-analytics disclosure.
findings.rationale: Incomplete sub-processor disclosure is a GDPR/CCPA completeness gap; lead.ts proves Resend + Turnstile usage.

findings.current: "...sign-in provider — Apple, Google, Meta / Instagram, or an email magic-link."
findings.suggested: Verify Meta/Instagram OAuth is actually offered; if not, remove it.
findings.rationale: Could be a false statement of a data source if Meta sign-in isn't implemented (not present in this repo).

source.note: needs confirmation against the app, not in this repo

findings.current: "These Terms are governed by the laws of the United States, and disputes are resolved in the courts of Delaware." (terms.astro:674; jurisdiction value terms.astro:111)
findings.suggested: Change 'laws of the United States' -> 'laws of the State of Delaware' to match the venue.
findings.rationale: Private commercial contracts are governed by state, not federal, law; the clause is internally mismatched (US law / Delaware venue).

findings.current: No cookie/analytics section. Site uses Cloudflare Web Analytics (cookieless) — correctly shows NO consent banner.
findings.suggested: Add a one-line disclosure: 'The website uses Cloudflare Web Analytics, a cookieless service; no tracking cookies are set, so no consent banner is shown.'
findings.rationale: GDPR Art. 13 expects disclosure of analytics processing even when no consent is required. (The absence of a banner is correct, not a defect.)

findings.current: "...grant us a limited, worldwide licence to process the image..." ('licence' British) while 'licensed' (American) is used elsewhere
findings.suggested: Change 'licence' -> 'license'.
findings.rationale: Spelling inconsistency in a legal document.

findings.current: "...users do not supply prompts — the prompt template is assembled server-side from your style selection."
findings.suggested: Drop the 'assembled server-side' implementation detail; it has no legal function.
findings.rationale: Leaked internal architecture detail in public legal copy.

findings.current: Neither legal doc names the contracting entity; only the footer says '© 2026 StencilMaker Inc.'
findings.suggested: Identify the entity at the top of both docs.
findings.rationale: Standard legal practice; clarifies who the contract is with.

findings.current: No refund / non-refundability clause (esp. Coinbase USDC credit packs, which are non-refundable on-chain).
findings.suggested: Add a refund clause referencing store policies for IAP and stating USDC credit packs are non-refundable once confirmed.
findings.rationale: Sets correct user expectations; required for store review compliance.

findings.current:
artists.astro:528

BLACK & GREY — DARK ART —...

inside a div NOT marked aria-hidden
findings.suggested: Add aria-hidden="true" to the wrapping div (artists.astro:523) or change the <h2> to a <span>.
findings.rationale: Decorative repeating text pollutes the screen-reader heading outline.

findings.current: form has novalidate; inputs lack aria-required/aria-invalid; JS validation errors aren't programmatically associated to fields
findings.suggested: Add aria-required="true" to name/email, set aria-invalid + aria-describedby='apply-status' on validation failure.
findings.rationale: Screen-reader users get the field focus but no programmatic error association.

findings.current: og:image:alt hardcoded to site tagline on /artists; /artists/apply has no BreadcrumbList JSON-LD
findings.suggested: Add artist-specific og:image:alt and a Home>Artists>Apply breadcrumb.
findings.rationale: Minor SEO polish for the new pages.

findings.current: artists.astro:32 follows Bruno's verbatim quoteCraft immediately with StencilMaker editorial copy with no visual break -> reads as if Bruno said it
findings.suggested: Separate the verbatim quote (— Bruno Moretti) from the editorial sentence with a figcaption/paragraph break.
findings.rationale: Ambiguous attribution of paid-partner quotes; the source notes require verbatim quotes stay clearly his.

links.note: FALSE POSITIVE — preconnect origin, not a real link; the css2 font URL is 200

ops.issue: Lead form AND artist-application form silently drop every submission in production
ops.detail: wrangler.toml has only the ASSETS binding active; DB (D1), LEADS_KV, and RESEND_* vars are all commented out. In lead.ts the no-binding path falls through to console.log and returns {ok:true}, so the user sees success while nothing is persisted or emailed.
ops.fix: Provision LEADS_KV (or D1) + RESEND_API_KEY/RESEND_FROM_EMAIL/LEAD_NOTIFY_EMAIL and uncomment the bindings before relying on either form.

ops.issue: Release process: production is behind master (stale deploy)
ops.detail: Latest commit d6f5df4 (featured-artist feature) is not live; /artists + /artists/apply 404 and homepage copy is older than source. Suggests Workers Builds isn't auto-deploying master, or the last manual `wrangler deploy` predates the feature.
ops.fix: Redeploy and verify the auto-deploy trigger (Workers Builds) is wired to master.

clean: Responsive: horizontal overflow = 0 on every page at 390px (no fixed-width canvas).

Geometry 'overlaps'/'clipped' flagged by the detector are FALSE POSITIVES: the Input/Output before-after slider (intentional), the collapsed mobile nav drawer, the off-canvas style-rail slides, and the oversized legal index numerals — confirmed clean in screenshots.
Security headers excellent: CSP, HSTS (preload), X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, COOP/CORP all present; no Set-Cookie.
Privacy: cookieless (Cloudflare Web Analytics) with NO consent banner = correct, not a defect.
Per-page SEO: unique title/description/canonical per page (incl. the not-yet-live artist pages); sitemap is sitemap-index.xml on the right host; robots sitemap host correct.
Pricing on the homepage matches consts.ts PRICING exactly (Free $0; Premium $7.99/mo, $59.99/yr, 37% save, 7-day trial; credit packs 550/1100/3000).
